question.text: Under SEBI ICDR, what is the minimum promoters' contribution required in an IPO as a percentage of post-issue capital?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 14 of SEBI ICDR mandates that promoters must contribute at least 20% of the post-issue capital in an IPO. This ensures promoters have meaningful "skin in the game." **The Trap:** 25% is the minimum public shareholding requirement under SCRR, often confused with the promoter contribution threshold. They serve different regulatory purposes. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) and NISM Series XV (Research Analyst) both reference promoter contribution norms when analyzing IPO structures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 20% must come from the promoter's own funds — not borrowed money. Verify this during due diligence to ensure genuine promoter commitment.
